commit.message:
feat: support dynamic matrix from job output via runtime expressions

Add support for generating matrix configurations at runtime from a
previous job's output, matching Azure DevOps' pattern:
  matrix: \\$[dependencies.Job.outputs['step.matrix']]\

A job can output a JSON string (via ##pipeline[setvariable]), and a
downstream job can use that JSON as its matrix definition. The runtime
expression is resolved after the upstream job completes, parsed from
JSON, validated, and fed into StrategyRunner for fan-out.

Changes:
- types/pipeline.ts: Allow JobStrategy.matrix to be string | Record
- parser/schema.ts: Accept z.union of record or string for matrix
- strategy-runner.ts: Guard against string matrix in expandStrategy()
- stage-runner.ts: Add resolveDynamicMatrix() — resolves \$[...] runtime
  expressions in matrix field, parses JSON, validates structure, coerces
  values to strings. Supports both same-stage (\$[dependencies...]) and
  cross-stage (\$[stageDependencies...]) expressions
- 4 new E2E tests: dynamic matrix fan-out, maxParallel, invalid JSON
  error, cross-stage dynamic matrix
- New example: 16-dynamic-matrix.yaml
- README: Added dynamic matrix documentation with examples

All 1,257 tests pass (998 unit + 72 integration + 187 E2E)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite CONTRIBUTING.md with three-tier contribution philosophy

Replaces PR-centric contributing guide with Casey's AI-first model:
- Level 1: File issues (feature requests, bug reports)
- Level 2: Submit detailed specs for agent implementation
- Level 3: Dogfood off master, file tagged issues

Reflects that EditLess uses specialized AI agents for all code changes,
making detailed specs more valuable than pull requests.

commit.message:
fix: patch app.asar during build so Codex launches outside MSIX

The patch-app-asar.mjs script existed but was never called in the
build pipeline.  Without the patch, process.windowsStore stays false
when running outside the Store container, causing Codex to fail at
launch.  Insert the patch step right after copying the app payload
to _internal/.

commit.message:
Fix interpreter async suspend DiagnosticIP calculation (#125282)

Fix #124044

The interpreter was storing an absolute address for `DiagnosticIP` in
`INTOP_HANDLE_CONTINUATION_SUSPEND`, but this field needs to be stored
as an offset and then relocated during `FinalizeMethodData` (similar to
other fields like `methodStartIP`).

This caused a crash in `ToString_Async` and other async exception
handling tests on Apple mobile with CoreCLR R2R and interpreter
fallback, because the diagnostic IP pointed to invalid memory.

**Changes:**
- `compiler.cpp`: Store `DiagnosticIP` as an offset from method code
start during emit, then add the final bytecode base address during
finalization.
- `simple-eh.cs`: Remove `ActiveIssue` attributes for the three tests
that were disabled due to this bug.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(water): caustics + dual-foam + improved specular

Inspiriert von threejs-caustics (martinRenou):
- Fake Caustics: min(noise1, noise2) * 2.8 ^ 3 erzeugt Stern-Muster
  die echte Lichtbrechung simulieren, nur in flachem Wasser sichtbar
- Dual-Schaum: breiter weicher Ring + enger heller Brechungslinie
- Stärkere Wellennormale (0.20 statt 0.15) fuer besseres Specular-Flimmern
- Specular: Exponent 160 + 0.85 Intensitaet (schrpfer, heller)
- Fresnel: 3.5 Exponent + 0.40 Mix (mehr Horizont-Reflexion)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite the bilingual README to prioritize the end-user experience:

- Package contents and runtime configuration follow
- Building from source and developer details moved to later sections
- Config field tables collapsed into <details> to reduce noise
- Removed duplicated architecture sections in favor of concise tables

commit.message:
refactor: optimize FieldError.String() performance (#7)

* perf: optimize FieldError.String() with string concatenation

Replaced fmt.Sprintf with direct string concatenation using the + operator
in the FieldError.String() method. This reduces the overhead of parsing
format strings and reflection, making error stringification more efficient.

Added a focused benchmark in config/errors_bench_test.go to measure the
performance improvement. While environment limitations prevented running
the benchmark, string concatenation is a well-known optimization in Go
for this use case.

Co-authored-by: cotocisternas <464819+cotocisternas@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update config/errors_bench_test.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Coto Cisternas <cotocisternas@gmail.com>

* perf: use strings.Builder in FieldError.String() and improve benchmark

---------

Signed-off-by: Coto Cisternas <cotocisternas@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: cotocisternas <464819+cotocisternas@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: properly escape backslashes and special chars in shellExample meta reconstruction

When shellExample has meta: true, the reconstructed TypeScript code now
correctly escapes all special characters in the cmd string:
- Backslashes: \ → \ (prevents \n, \t etc. being misinterpreted as escape sequences)
- Single quotes: ' → \' (already worked, preserved)
- Newlines: newline → \n
- Carriage returns: CR → \r

Added escapeForSingleQuotedString() helper function.

Also adds tests for:
- describe() names with apostrophes and double quotes (parsing and rendering)
- shellExample meta mode with special characters in cmd strings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(water): remove vertex clamping to fix chunk seams

Chunk-Kanten waren sichtbar weil Unterwasser-Vertices auf Y=0 geclampt
wurden -> flache Polygon-Dreiecke an Chunk-Grenzen.

Fix: Vertices bleiben an echter Position (Meeresboden).
Wasser-Wellen nur noch in Fragment-Normale (Noise-Perturbation),
kein Vertex-Displacement. Alpha=1.0 (kein transparentes Blending
zwischen Chunks mehr).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Barbecube25 <212209420+Barbecube25@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace s[i] character indexing with read_byte on arm64

json_get_value, json_escape, http_url_decode, http_read_body_small,
and http_read_request all used s[i] character indexing which produces
incorrect results on linux_arm64. Replace with read_byte() for reads
and _byte_to_char() for single-char string creation. Also use
copy_bytes_raw() for bulk buffer reads instead of char-by-char.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace s[i] character indexing with read_byte/store8 on arm64

net_read_line, net_read_all, net_poll_read, and net_bytes_available
all used s[i] character indexing which produces incorrect results on
linux_arm64 cross-compiled binaries. Replace with read_byte() for
reads and store8() for writes. Also fix null-byte buffers in
net_poll_read and net_bytes_available with make_buffer().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restore React-islands homepage and modernized UI stack (#52)

* Modernize blog site with React island, updated styles, and improved components

- Install react, react-dom, @astrojs/react dependencies
- Add @astrojs/react integration to astro.config.mjs
- Update SITE_TITLE and SITE_DESCRIPTION in consts.ts
- Replace global.css with comprehensive modern CSS featuring custom properties, animations, cards, buttons, dark mode, and accessibility utilities
- Redesign Header.astro with sticky glassmorphism nav, scroll shadow, aria attributes, and social link animations
- Redesign Footer.astro with grid layout, brand tagline, nav links, and social icons
- Update HeaderLink.astro with animated underline pseudo-element on hover/active
- Update BlogPost.astro with figure/figcaption for hero image, lazy loading, and slide-up animation
- Update BaseHead.astro to add theme-color meta tag
- Create HeroSection.tsx React island with entrance animations, gradient orbs, and CTA button
- Redesign index.astro to use HeroSection client:load island and 3-column post card grid

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scroll event listener cleanup with AbortController

Use AbortController to properly clean up the scroll listener when the
page becomes hidden, preventing potential memory leaks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use React state for hover effects; add animation-delay to reduced-motion

* style: add editorial social preview and polish README front page

* Restore modern static-first site shell and AI crawl controls

* Fix markdown layout metadata resolution

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature/improve lf blockingqueue (#243)

* Initial plan

* Fix atomic memory orderings in lock-free blocking queue

- pushImpl: use memory_order_release (not acquire) for the m_next CAS
  that publishes newNode to consumers; add explicit relaxed failure order
- pushImpl: use memory_order_release/relaxed for both tail-advance CAS calls
- pushImpl: reduce m_total_push_count fetch_add from seq_cst to relaxed
- popOptional: add explicit memory_order_relaxed failure arg to tail CAS
- stop: add explicit memory_order_release to m_tail.store(nullptr)
- retireNode: replace implicit seq_cst m_retired_next assignment with
  explicit store(relaxed); CAS release provides required ordering
- InFlightGuard: reduce m_epochInFlightCount fetch_add/sub from seq_cst
  to acq_rel; add clarifying comments throughout

Co-authored-by: cheebinhoh <34779770+cheebinhoh@users.noreply.github.com>

* improve

* improve

* Update include/dmn-blockingqueue-lf.hpp

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(water): terrain-shader water rendering

Wasser wird jetzt direkt im Terrain-Shader gerendert (vHeight < 0).
Keine separate Water-Plane mehr - Küstenlinie folgt pixelgenau der Heightmap.

terrain.vert.glsl:
- Unterwasser-Vertices einfrieren auf Y=0 + kleine Sinus-Wellen
- vWorldPos varying für Fragment-Shader

terrain.frag.glsl:
- vHeight < 0: Wasser (tiefenbasierte Farbe, Noise-Schaum, Specular, Fresnel)
- vHeight >= 0: Land unverändert
- Neue uniforms: uTime, uCameraPos, uNoiseTex

WorldManager.js:
- Stencil entfernt (nicht mehr benötigt)
- Noise-Textur in init() laden (_noiseTex)
- uTime/uCameraPos/uNoiseTex uniforms in loadChunk()
- update() aktualisiert uTime

WaterManager.js:
- Vereinfacht zu statischer Horizont-Fallback-Plane (200km, MeshBasic)
- depthWrite=false, renderOrder=-1

Aethelgard.js:
- Water-Shader nicht mehr laden
- WaterManager ohne Shader instanziieren

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: modernize typing annotations and improve code quality

- Replace deprecated typing.List/Dict/Optional imports with Python 3.10+
  built-in generics (list[X], dict[X,Y], X | None) across all backend
  modules: schemas, services, controllers, repositories
- Fix unused exception variable in AIDiagnoser.suggest() (removed as e
  since logger.exception() already captures the full traceback)
- Add return type annotations to ai-engine/main.py endpoint functions
  (health, diagnose, suggest)
- Add docstrings to Pydantic models in ai-engine/main.py
- Type-annotate the _diagnoser singleton in ai-engine/main.py

All 45 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add governance stack burst

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Return default value when entity ttl is retrieved with entity cache enabled set to false (#3156)

## Why make this change?

Closes https://github.com/Azure/data-api-builder/issues/3148

Test change to help the pipeline pass relates to
https://github.com/Azure/data-api-builder/issues/2992

## What is this change?

Within the code paths for executing a REST request with the
RuntimeCacheOptions enabled, we would attempt to get the entity cache
ttl, however, if the entity had its cache options not enabled this would
result in an exception, leading to a bad request returned to the user.
However, the code path was already safeguarded by checking if we have
entity cache enabled before executing a cache-based response, and so we
did not need the exception when getting the entity cache ttl, and can
safely return null to indicate that there is no cache ttl.

## How was this tested?

A simple regression test is added that validates the new behavior.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Aniruddh Munde <anmunde@microsoft.com>

Make smart-start menu context-aware based on PR draft state and worktree (#102)

* chore: scaffold branch for #101

* feat: make smart-start menu context-aware based on PR draft state

- Extract isDraft flag from PR info
- Check worktree presence for the branch
- Show "Start implementation" for draft PR without worktree
- Show "Continue working" for draft PR with worktree
- Hide "Address reviews" and "Merge PR" for draft PRs
- Keep all three options for ready (non-draft) PRs
- Use dynamic (label, callable) tuples for menu building
- Add 3 new test cases for draft PR scenarios
- All 9 tests pass, linting and type checking pass

Closes #101

* fix: preserve caller's provider in merge wrapper and guard missing worktree

- Pass the already-resolved `provider` from `smart_start()` into
  `_run_merge_pr_wrapper` instead of re-calling `load_config()`/
  `get_provider()` inside the inner closure, which could resolve against
  the wrong cwd when `project_root` differs from the shell cwd.
- Add an explicit `worktree_path is None` guard in `_impl` that emits a
  warning before calling `_merge_pr`, so users see a clear message that
  local cleanup will be skipped (instead of silently skipping it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(admin+e2e): direct post-login navigation + Results regex + E2E hardening

- apps/admin/src/app/login/page.tsx: after login, decode JWT client-side
  and use resolveAdminLanding(permissions, isSuperAdmin) to navigate
  directly to /dashboard for super-admin, instead of router.replace('/')
  which relied on middleware redirect that was unreliable in standalone mode.
  Fixes all 30 failing admin E2E tests (redirected to /account instead of /dashboard).

- apps/e2e/tests/operator/01-navigation.spec.ts: fix sidebar Results link
  regex from /results/i to /result/i — sidebar shows 'Result Entry' not 'Results'.

- apps/e2e/tests/05-operator-workflow.spec.ts: assert labOrders exist and
  both result submission and verify commands succeed (< 300 status).
  Converts silent failures to explicit test failures with clear diagnostics.

- apps/e2e/tests/12-operator-account.spec.ts: add explicit
  expect(displayName).toBeVisible({timeout:15_000}) before inputValue()
  to wait for profile to load from API before interacting with form.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: send push notifications from SMS ingest and email sync

Previously, push notifications were only sent during manual package refresh.
Now SMS ingest, CSV import, and Gmail sync all trigger push notifications
when packages are created or status changes.
